scope.summary: Non-JS async runtime obligation attribution now requires stronger runtime identity evidence before Python asyncio, Rust aggregate macro, or Swift Task surfaces receive shared scheduling/task/aggregate labels.

capability_alignment.capability: runtime API identity proof before obligation attribution
not_a_feature_list: No new SourceProtocolKind, SourceCallKind, or public semantic-pack API was added.
No exact admission path was opened for Python, Rust, or Swift async runtime APIs.
Python asyncio aliases remain outside this slice until alias-backed occurrence proof is modeled.
Rust unqualified join!/try_join!/select! macros are no longer priced or reported as runtime aggregates by name alone.
reused_capabilities: shared task and async aggregate obligation labels

guarded_surfaces.guard: The receiver must be the import-backed asyncio namespace, with no same-file asyncio parameter or assignment shadow.
Aggregate macro reporting is limited to qualified tokio/futures/futures_util macro paths.
Task reporting requires the Task root to be unshadowed by a visible same-file definition.

assertions: shadowed Python asyncio.gather does not report async-aggregate-all-completion-contract
unimported Python asyncio.sleep does not report timer-scheduling-contract
unqualified Rust join! does not report async-aggregate-all-completion-contract
unqualified Rust select! does not report async-aggregate-first-completion-contract
shadowed Swift Task and Task.detached do not report task-spawn-scheduling-contract

corpus_pricing_delta.reason: The audit now prices only qualified tokio/futures/futures_util aggregate macros, matching the runtime-boundary reporter.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: The reporter now rejects common shadowed or unqualified false runtime identities. Exact recovery still requires proof for scheduling, cancellation, lifecycle, result-channel, exception, and runtime-specific callback/effect behavior.
